steps.title: Create and test the first graduate futures evidence pack
steps.summary: This step creates the first working version of the evidence pack that course teams will use to understand their students' graduate futures. It starts with the evidence itself: what we can include, what needs careful interpretation, and where the data has limits. It then tests whether the pack helps colleagues see a fair and useful picture of what their course enables for students.

outputs.title: Data specification
outputs.summary: A clear specification of the sources, fields, owners, caveats, update cycles, suppression rules, segmentation and responsible-use principles for the first pack.

outputs.title: Interpretation approach
outputs.summary: An agreed approach to interpreting destinations, outcomes, comparator evidence, student aspiration data and course context.

outputs.title: First working graduate futures evidence pack
outputs.summary: A first visual and narrative pack, including core data visualisations and explanatory material for testing with colleagues.

outputs.title: Early user feedback
outputs.summary: Feedback from selected academic and professional services users on whether the pack is understandable, credible, fair and useful.

outputs.title: Improvement list for the next version
outputs.summary: A short, prioritised list of changes needed before the pack is shared more widely.

existingCapacity.owner: Mark Peace, Rachel Kershaw and Daniel Robson
existingCapacity.contribution: Maintain the minimum plan, decisions, access arrangements, recruitment controls and delivery coordination until project management capacity begins.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: Step 1 could proceed without clear ownership of immediate decisions, access and dependencies.

existingCapacity.contribution: Direct the evidence-pack prototype, interpretation approach and user-testing standard.
Lead the Careers contribution, manage the internship placement and coordinate data, pack-production and analytical input.

existingCapacity.role: Data Manager, Careers and Student Success
existingCapacity.contribution: Provide operational data expertise and support pack production, refresh, analytical quality and documentation. The named contribution and availability will be confirmed by Rachel Kershaw.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: The prototype may not establish a sustainable or operationally credible production process.

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide the initial part of up to three working days of advice on Microsoft Fabric setup, access and technical approach.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: The prototype may be built without a supportable technical and handover route.

existingCapacity.contribution: Three to five academics from varied faculties and course contexts test whether the first pack is understandable, credible, fair and useful.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: Testing will not adequately represent the academic users expected to use the pack.

newInvestment.rationale: A capped budget for a full-time, six-week placement of 210 hours, including wages at the applicable London Living Wage, employer on-costs and contingency. The intern will produce and test the first course-level pack and document a reusable production process.
newInvestment.riskIfMissing: The first pack and reusable production method would need to be assembled from existing capacity, reducing pace and depth of testing.

newInvestment.rationale: Support controlled prototyping of advanced qualitative analysis using the UK Standard Skills Classification as the shared skills language.
newInvestment.riskIfMissing: The prototype would use a more limited manual analytical approach.

enablingConditions.condition: Complete a short internship recruitment process led jointly by Mark Peace and Rachel Kershaw.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The six-week placement cannot begin in the required summer window.

enablingConditions.condition: Provide a Microsoft Fabric project workspace, suitable capacity and appropriate permissions for Rachel Kershaw, the intern and technical contributors.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The pack cannot be developed and handed over through the intended institutional platform.

enablingConditions.condition: Confirm controlled access to Graduate Outcomes and core employability data, with agreed definitions, caveats, suppression and refresh arrangements.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The first pack cannot provide a credible course-level view.

enablingConditions.condition: Identify the Enrolment Data Owner in the Student Success Directorate and agree course populations, fields, permissions, caveats and refresh arrangements.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Course-level coverage and segmentation may be incomplete or inconsistent.

enablingConditions.condition: Use an approved LLM environment with permitted data, controlled access, human validation and a recorded information-governance and AI-assurance route.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: LLM-assisted analysis could create information-governance, quality or responsible-use risks.

decisions.title: Confirm evidence for the first version
decisions.summary: Agree what evidence is robust enough to include and what should be held back because it is incomplete, misleading or too difficult to interpret responsibly.

decisions.title: Confirm early testers
decisions.summary: Agree which course, department, faculty and professional services colleagues should test the first version.

decisions.title: Confirm caveats and interpretation guidance
decisions.summary: Agree what explanation must sit alongside the data so colleagues can interpret it responsibly.

decisions.title: Confirm September 2026 scope
decisions.summary: Agree what the pack needs to do by September 2026 and what can wait for later versions.

decisions.title: Confirm internship recruitment and access arrangements
decisions.summary: Confirm the placement brief, selection process, supervision, start date, Fabric workspace and data access. Funding of up to £4,000 from Student Futures Programme residuals is already confirmed.

risks.title: The first pack looks too definitive
risks.mitigation: Use visible caveats, clear development status and testing language.

risks.title: Comparator evidence encourages league-table thinking
risks.mitigation: Frame comparators as context for reflection, not ranking.

risks.title: Uncertainty is hidden
risks.mitigation: Make data gaps, small numbers, suppression and volatility visible.

risks.title: Testing misses the academic user perspective
risks.mitigation: Include colleagues who will actually need to use the pack in course reviews.

steps.title: Share the first course packs and learn from use
steps.summary: This step shares the first course-level packs with selected colleagues and learns from how they use them. The aim is to check whether the packs help course teams recognise strengths, ask useful questions and describe their students' graduate futures with confidence, and to identify improvements to the evidence, language and guidance.

outputs.title: Second version of the course evidence packs
outputs.summary: Course-facing packs improved from the first round of testing.

outputs.title: Short user guide
outputs.summary: A concise guide to help colleagues read and use the packs responsibly.

outputs.title: Faculty conversations
outputs.summary: Faculty-level discussions about what the evidence is showing, where it is useful and where it needs care.

outputs.title: Early institutional summary
outputs.summary: A short summary for Education Committee or the agreed senior education forum, drawing out early themes and questions.

outputs.title: Learning from use
outputs.summary: A record of what colleagues find useful, confusing, sensitive or missing, to inform the next version of the packs and the self-run course sense-making workshops.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own interpretation, conclusions and the early institutional synthesis, and chair the Reference Group.
Oversee pack production, analytical synthesis and the Careers contribution to early sharing and learning.

existingCapacity.role: Data Manager, Careers and Student Success
existingCapacity.contribution: Produce the next pack version and respond to data, quality and interpretation issues identified through early use.
Maintain the plan, risks, decisions and dependencies; coordinate workstreams and approvals; provide Reference Group secretariat; and record conditions and issue resolution.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: Daniel, Rachel and Mark would continue carrying coordination activity that should sit with a project manager.

existingCapacity.contribution: Use early course packs and provide structured feedback on clarity, usefulness, responsible representation and support needs.
Use existing faculty business-partner relationships to support interpretation and route early questions without creating a separate faculty engagement structure.

One named representative from every faculty, collectively drawing on Vice-Deans Education, Associate Directors of Education and Deputy Vice-Deans for Student Success, validates usefulness, credibility and responsible representation through asynchronous review.

enablingConditions.condition: Complete the first asynchronous Reference Group review and record approval conditions, material issues and their resolution.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The pack may proceed without credible institution-wide validation.

enablingConditions.condition: Separate analytical synthesis from institutional judgement: Careers produces the analytical synthesis, the Project Manager coordinates inputs and evidence, and Daniel Robson owns interpretation and conclusions.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Institutional reporting could have unclear authorship or accountability.

decisions.title: Confirm sharing route
decisions.summary: Agree how the first course packs should be shared and with whom.

decisions.title: Confirm faculty and institutional conversations
decisions.summary: Agree which faculty or institutional conversations need to happen before wider use.

decisions.title: Confirm Reference Group validation outcome
decisions.summary: Daniel Robson confirms whether the Reference Group's conditions and material issues have been resolved sufficiently for the framework to proceed to Education Executive approval.

decisions.title: Confirm interpretation level
decisions.summary: Agree how much interpretation should be included in the packs themselves.

decisions.title: Confirm handling of sensitive findings
decisions.summary: Agree how sensitive or surprising findings should be discussed without creating anxiety, defensiveness or premature conclusions.

risks.title: Sharing feels like performance reporting
risks.mitigation: Use developmental framing and make clear that the packs are intended to help colleagues understand and evidence their graduate futures story.

risks.title: The packs are useful only with heavy explanation
risks.mitigation: Use a short guide and test whether colleagues can understand the core messages directly.

risks.title: Christmas 2026 becomes overloaded
risks.mitigation: Keep the scope focused and separate essential outputs from improvements that can wait.

steps.title: Design the self-run course sense-making workshop
steps.summary: This step designs the self-run sense-making workshop that accompanies each course pack. It provides clear prompts, guidance and a two-sided Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page so course teams can interpret the evidence, agree a graduate futures narrative and record any proportionate action or supported conclusion.

outputs.title: Self-run sense-making workshop plan
outputs.summary: A practical workshop plan that course teams can run themselves, with a clear sequence from evidence to claim, questions and action.

outputs.title: Sense-making prompts
outputs.summary: A small set of prompts to help course teams explore patterns, strengths, questions and uncertainties in the evidence.

outputs.title: Workshop guidance
outputs.summary: Plain-language guidance for the person convening the workshop, including timing, tone, participation and handling uncertainty.

outputs.title: Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page template
outputs.summary: A two-sided course-level template that captures an evidence-informed graduate futures claim, supporting evidence, caveats, strengths to amplify, and any action, question or supported no-action conclusion arising from the review.

outputs.title: Responsible-use guidance
outputs.summary: Guidance on uncertainty, small numbers, sensitive comparisons and differences between disciplinary contexts.

existingCapacity.owner: King's Academy; nomination requested by Daniel Robson
existingCapacity.contribution: Lead the educational design of the self-run workshop and templates and shape the evaluation approach.
existingCapacity.periodNeeded: Design through completion of the pilot
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: The workshop may not be proportionate, academically credible or suitable for self-run use.

existingCapacity.role: Careers Workshop, Pilot and Mobilisation Lead
existingCapacity.owner: Careers and Employability Service; nomination by Rachel Kershaw
existingCapacity.contribution: Co-design the discussion pack, connect the workshop to Careers practice and prepare the Careers contribution to piloting and mobilisation.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: The workshop may not connect effectively to the evidence, Careers support or mobilisation route.

existingCapacity.role: Data Manager, Careers and Student Success
existingCapacity.contribution: Ensure the workshop and template reflect the evidence, analytical caveats and feasible pack-production process.
Coordinate design inputs, decisions, dependencies, testing and approvals.
Test whether the workshop and two-sided template are clear, proportionate and realistic in varied course contexts.
Provide the first of three two-hour specialist advisory and assurance touchpoints, focused on the workshop design and Curriculum Education Review route.

existingCapacity.owner: Director of Academic Quality to nominate
existingCapacity.contribution: Advise on CER categories, workflow, guidance, reporting and cross-referencing.

enablingConditions.condition: Daniel Robson secures a named King's Academy Academic Development Lead and Rachel Kershaw nominates the Careers Workshop, Pilot and Mobilisation Lead.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Design accountability and coordinated specialist input will remain unclear.

enablingConditions.condition: Design the Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page as the initial course-owned record, with actions, questions and support needs transferable into CER where applicable.
enablingConditions.owner: King's Academy lead, Careers lead and Operational CER Contact
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The review output may duplicate existing quality processes or fail to create a usable action record.

decisions.title: Confirm purpose of the conversation
decisions.summary: Agree what a useful course review should produce.

decisions.title: Confirm facilitation route
decisions.summary: Agree who is best placed to facilitate conversations locally.

decisions.title: Confirm level of structure
decisions.summary: Agree how much structure is helpful without making the conversation feel scripted.

decisions.title: Confirm Claim and Action on a Page and CER interface
decisions.summary: Agree what the two-sided course-owned template records and how resulting actions, questions and support needs transfer into CER or an agreed alternative route.

decisions.title: Confirm follow-up expectation
decisions.summary: Agree what should happen after a course has had the conversation.

risks.title: The conversation feels diagnostic or corrective
risks.mitigation: Design prompts around course graduate futures claim, evidence, questions and action, not deficit identification.

risks.title: The materials become too heavy
risks.mitigation: Use a light core structure, with optional deeper prompts where needed.

risks.title: The model does not fit different disciplines
risks.mitigation: Test with colleagues from varied course contexts and leave space for local interpretation.

steps.title: Pilot and refine the pack and self-run workshop
steps.summary: This step tests the pack, self-run workshop and Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page with early course partners. Their feedback is used to improve the evidence, wording, guidance and templates before the first wider rollout.

outputs.title: Early course pilots
outputs.summary: A small number of real course pilots using the evidence pack, self-run workshop and two-sided template.

outputs.title: Feedback from academic colleagues
outputs.summary: Feedback on the pack, prompts, facilitation approach and overall usefulness of the process.

outputs.title: Examples of course insight and graduate futures claims
outputs.summary: Examples of the kinds of insights, questions, possible actions and graduate futures stories the approach can generate.

outputs.title: Learning record
outputs.summary: A record of where the evidence was useful, where it was unclear and where further explanation or support was needed.

outputs.title: Improved graduate futures evidence pack
outputs.summary: A revised pack that better supports course graduate futures claim, responsible interpretation and practical reflection.

outputs.title: Updated interpretation guidance
outputs.summary: Clearer guidance on responsible use, caveats and points that need better explanation.

outputs.title: Refined self-run sense-making workshop plan
outputs.summary: Revised prompts and facilitation notes based on early course use.

outputs.title: Refined Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page template
outputs.summary: A tested two-sided template for capturing course-owned graduate futures claims and proportionate actions or conclusions, refined through early course use.

outputs.title: Plan for the first rollout
outputs.summary: A clear plan for the focal courses in the first rollout, including the rationale for their selection and the support available.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own the pilot standard, interpretation of findings and decision on changes required before wider use.
Oversee Careers participation, pack refinement and operational learning.
Coordinate three to five pilots, feedback, debriefs, evidence, decisions and issue resolution.
Support or observe selected pilots, shape evaluation and lead workshop refinements.

existingCapacity.role: Careers Workshop, Pilot and Mobilisation Lead
existingCapacity.contribution: Coordinate Careers support, observe selected pilots and refine the workshop and support offer.

existingCapacity.role: Data Manager, Careers and Student Success
existingCapacity.contribution: Revise the pack and production process in response to pilot learning.
Provide a named linked consultant and up to half a day of support for each pilot departmental setting.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: Pilot teams may not receive enough help to interpret unfamiliar or sensitive evidence.

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide the mid-build part of up to three working days of technical review and assurance.
Provide the second two-hour advisory and assurance touchpoint after the pilot.

newInvestment.item: LLM usage for pilot and refinement
newInvestment.rationale: Refine the controlled qualitative analysis method and test its usefulness in real course contexts.
newInvestment.riskIfMissing: Pilot refinement would rely on more limited manual analysis.

enablingConditions.condition: Secure three to five pilot courses from varied contexts. Course teams decide their own local participants and arrange local time.
enablingConditions.owner: Daniel Robson with faculty education leadership
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The pilot will not provide enough evidence about usability across different course contexts.

enablingConditions.condition: Use human validation for every LLM-assisted interpretation and retain a clear record of source evidence, caveats and changes.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Automated interpretation could be accepted without sufficient subject or contextual judgement.

enablingConditions.condition: Complete the second Reference Group review after material pilot changes, or record why no further review is required.
enablingConditions.owner: Daniel Robson and Borrowed Project Manager
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Material changes may not receive adequate institution-wide validation.

decisions.title: Confirm three to five pilot courses
decisions.summary: Select a small, varied pilot group for learning value, readiness and context, rather than because courses are judged to be problematic.

decisions.title: Confirm invitation and briefing approach
decisions.summary: Agree how early partners should be invited and briefed so the work feels collegiate and constructive.

decisions.title: Confirm learning mix
decisions.summary: Agree what mix of course types, disciplines and student profiles would give the most useful learning.

decisions.title: Confirm essential changes
decisions.summary: Agree which changes are essential before wider use and which can wait.

decisions.title: Confirm standard and flexible elements
decisions.summary: Agree which parts of the approach should be consistent across King's and which can flex locally.

decisions.title: Confirm readiness for Education Executive approval
decisions.summary: Agree whether the pack, workshop and Claim and Action on a Page have met the pilot acceptance criteria and are ready for the institutional approval gate.

decisions.title: Confirm rationale language
decisions.summary: Agree how to explain the rationale for the next groups of courses in a fair and constructive way.

decisions.title: Confirm how findings are shared
decisions.summary: Agree how learning can be shared without exposing, labelling or over-interpreting individual courses.

risks.title: Early partners are read as being judged
risks.mitigation: Choose and invite partners for learning value, not because they are seen as problems.

risks.title: The model is locked too early
risks.mitigation: Use early feedback to make real changes rather than simply confirm the original design.

risks.title: Early examples imply a single correct model
risks.mitigation: Use examples to show variation, not prescribe one course graduate futures claim.

risks.title: The approach works only in unusually engaged contexts
risks.mitigation: Select early partners that provide useful variation in discipline, student profile and course context.

risks.title: Local flexibility makes the approach incoherent
risks.mitigation: Define a light common core with space for local adaptation.

risks.title: The next phase feels like targeting
risks.mitigation: Explain the rationale as focused support, story development and institutional learning.

steps.title: Complete the first rollout with focal courses
steps.summary: This step agrees the focal courses for the first rollout, distributes their packs and supports them to complete the self-run sense-making workshop. Each course records its graduate futures claim and any action or supported conclusion on the two-sided template. Selection is transparent and avoids presenting inclusion as a judgement on course quality.

outputs.title: Selection approach for focal courses
outputs.summary: A transparent approach for agreeing the focal courses in the first rollout without implying that selection is a judgement on course quality.

outputs.title: First rollout completed
outputs.summary: Focal courses receive their packs and complete the self-run sense-making workshop, with additional support where needed.

outputs.title: Completed Graduate Futures Claims and Actions on a Page
outputs.summary: Course-owned records of graduate futures claims, supporting evidence, caveats and any agreed action or supported conclusion.

outputs.title: Practical local actions
outputs.summary: A small number of actions that course teams can take forward locally.

outputs.title: Examples of distinctive course value
outputs.summary: Evidence-informed examples of effective practice or distinctive graduate futures stories, where course teams are comfortable for these to be shared.

outputs.title: Faculty-level learning
outputs.summary: Common themes, recurring questions and areas where further support may be needed.

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide 0.1 FTE through June 2027 and 0.05 FTE from July 2027, own the prioritisation proposal and confirm the approved rollout.
Establish the feasible Careers capacity envelope, oversee pack production and co-develop the prioritisation proposal.
Provide 0.2 FTE through June 2027 and 0.1 FTE from July 2027 to coordinate prioritisation, rollout, dependencies, coverage, decisions and exceptions.

existingCapacity.role: Data Manager, Careers and Student Success
existingCapacity.contribution: Produce, check and distribute course-level packs in departmental bundles and maintain the production register.

existingCapacity.role: Careers Workshop, Pilot and Mobilisation Lead
existingCapacity.contribution: Coordinate the Careers contribution to focal-area mobilisation and support linked consultants.
Provide up to half a day of support for each selected focal departmental bundle through existing faculty business-partner relationships.
existingCapacity.riskIfMissing: The feasible focal-area scale may need to be reduced.

newInvestment.item: LLM usage for first rollout and pre-BAU pack production
newInvestment.rationale: Support pack production and controlled qualitative analysis before the method moves into BAU. Unused amounts from earlier phases may carry forward.
newInvestment.riskIfMissing: The first rollout may require more manual analysis or a smaller production envelope.

enablingConditions.condition: Careers and the Project Manager establish the feasible pack-production and Careers Consultant capacity envelope before focal areas are proposed.
enablingConditions.owner: Rachel Kershaw and Borrowed Project Manager
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The institution could approve a rollout that cannot be supported with available capacity.

enablingConditions.condition: Daniel Robson and Rachel Kershaw prepare an evidence-informed proposal using value, readiness, data, strategic relevance, diversity, support and non-punitive selection criteria.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The rationale for focal-area selection may be unclear or perceived as punitive.

enablingConditions.condition: Education Executive, involving Vice-Deans Education, approves the focal areas and departmental bundles for the first rollout.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The focal-area decision will lack the required institutional and faculty authority.

enablingConditions.condition: Packs are produced and coverage is counted at course level, while mobilisation and distribution may be organised through departmental bundles.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Coverage reporting and the practical unit of mobilisation could become confused.

decisions.title: Confirm the feasible capacity envelope
decisions.summary: Rachel Kershaw, Careers and the Project Manager confirm how many departmental bundles can be supported through available pack-production and Careers Consultant capacity.

decisions.title: Approve focal areas and departmental bundles
decisions.summary: Education Executive, involving Vice-Deans Education, approves the actual focal areas using the evidence-informed proposal and records any conditions or deferred areas.

decisions.title: Confirm the support offer
decisions.summary: Agree the pack, guidance, Careers Consultant support and escalation route available to focal departments, without centrally prescribing local programme participation.

decisions.title: Confirm coverage and exception recording
decisions.summary: Agree how course-level production, distribution, completed review and documented exceptions will be recorded within departmental bundles.

risks.title: Selection feels punitive
risks.mitigation: Use a transparent shared process and include strengths, distinctive practice and learning value as well as support needs.

risks.title: Course groups feel labelled
risks.mitigation: Frame the work as focused support for course development and story strengthening.

risks.title: Course actions become performative paperwork
risks.mitigation: Keep actions small, owned and routed through existing course, department or faculty routes.

steps.title: Move the packs and review process into normal ongoing practice
steps.summary: This step moves ongoing production and use of the graduate futures packs into normal institutional practice. It confirms who owns the data and pack production, how often evidence is refreshed, where colleagues get help with interpretation, and how agreed course actions enter existing quality enhancement monitoring.

outputs.title: Evidence refresh rhythm
outputs.summary: An agreed rhythm for refreshing the evidence packs, including which data can be updated regularly and which data needs longer cycles.

outputs.title: Ongoing ownership model
outputs.summary: Ownership for maintaining the data packs, dashboard, narrative guidance, interpretation materials and review process.

outputs.title: Update route
outputs.summary: A route for updating the packs as data sources, student patterns, course needs or institutional priorities change.

outputs.title: Place in normal planning and review
outputs.summary: An agreed place for the data packs and review process in existing course, department, faculty or institutional planning conversations.

outputs.title: Quality enhancement monitoring handoff
outputs.summary: A clear route for agreed graduate futures actions to enter existing quality enhancement monitoring.

outputs.title: Ongoing feedback process
outputs.summary: A simple process for gathering feedback from colleagues who use the packs and review process over time.

existingCapacity.role: Careers and Employability Workstream Lead and senior BAU owner
existingCapacity.contribution: Provide senior accountability for the evidence-pack service and agree the BAU operating model.

existingCapacity.owner: Data Manager, Careers and Student Success
existingCapacity.contribution: Own pack production, data refresh, analytical quality, documentation and continuity in BAU.

existingCapacity.role: Careers Workshop, Pilot and Mobilisation Lead
existingCapacity.contribution: Support transition of the workshop, guidance and mobilisation approach into normal Careers practice.
Coordinate the capacity checkpoint, handover evidence, decisions, assurance and closure conditions.
Confirm CER categories, workflow, guidance, reporting and the documented alternative where CER does not apply.
Provide the final two-hour advisory and assurance touchpoint on BAU and monitoring handoff.
Provide the final part of up to three working days of technical assurance and Fabric handover.

enablingConditions.condition: Complete a formal Careers data-capacity checkpoint using evidence about production time, automation, refresh workload, analytical queries, pilot support and LLM-assisted analysis.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: BAU may be accepted without evidence that existing capacity is sufficient.

enablingConditions.condition: Document the Fabric workspace, data permissions, publishing, maintenance, refresh, quality-assurance, LLM and information-governance arrangements.
enablingConditions.owner: Careers and Employability Service with Data Services
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The service may depend on undocumented access or project-specific knowledge.

enablingConditions.condition: Use CER as the default route for actions, questions and support needs, with a documented alternative where CER does not apply.
enablingConditions.owner: Academic Quality and Careers and Employability Service
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Review outputs may not enter an established ownership and monitoring process.

enablingConditions.condition: Assign resulting actions to the locally designated curriculum owner, recognising that curriculum ownership varies between faculties.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Actions may be assigned to a role that does not hold curriculum ownership locally.

decisions.title: Confirm the Careers BAU operating model
decisions.summary: Confirm Rachel Kershaw as senior accountable Careers owner, the Data Manager role as operational pack owner, and the production, refresh, access and support arrangements.

decisions.title: Confirm whether existing data capacity is sufficient
decisions.summary: Use the post-prototype and pilot capacity evidence to decide whether the agreed BAU service can be sustained without additional analytical capacity.

decisions.title: Confirm the CER and alternative action routes
decisions.summary: Agree how actions, questions and support needs move into CER, how faculty education committees monitor follow-through, and what route applies where CER is not suitable.

decisions.title: Confirm technical and assurance handover
decisions.summary: Accept the documented Fabric, data, quality, LLM and information-governance arrangements and any outstanding conditions.

risks.title: The packs become static dashboards
risks.mitigation: Confirm ownership for data, visualisation, narrative guidance, interpretation and the review process, not just dashboard refresh.

risks.title: The transfer is only technical
risks.mitigation: Include both technical ownership of the data packs and academic ownership of how they are used in review and enhancement.

risks.title: The review process creates a parallel process
risks.mitigation: Embed it in existing quality assurance, planning and enhancement cycles.

steps.title: Complete the second rollout across remaining courses
steps.summary: This step extends the pack and self-run workshop to all remaining in-scope courses. Additional support is available where needed. The resulting claims and actions are then synthesised to identify institutional strengths, recurring questions and support needs.

outputs.title: Supported reviews for remaining courses
outputs.summary: Supported graduate futures conversations where direct support is needed.

outputs.title: Self-run review route for remaining courses
outputs.summary: A route for courses able to use the evidence pack, guidance and workshop materials without a supported review.

outputs.title: Institution-wide review completion
outputs.summary: Confirmation that the remaining agreed courses have either completed a supported review or have been enabled to complete a self-review.

outputs.title: Institutional graduate futures synthesis
outputs.summary: A synthesis of themes across courses, departments and faculties, including strengths, recurring questions and areas where further support may be needed.

outputs.title: Recommendations for related King's Edge work
outputs.summary: Recommendations for curriculum enhancement, skills, opportunity, evidence and student-facing story work.

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide light oversight, resolve exceptions and own final interpretation, conclusions and deliverable synthesis.
Provide senior Careers oversight of broader rollout and BAU pack production.
Track production, distribution, review completion and exceptions; coordinate reporting, synthesis inputs, handover and closure.

existingCapacity.owner: Data Manager, Careers and Student Success
existingCapacity.contribution: Maintain and distribute the course-level packs through the agreed BAU process.

existingCapacity.role: Careers Workshop, Pilot and Mobilisation Lead
existingCapacity.contribution: Provide light coordination and support during the broader rollout.
Remain available on request through normal faculty business-partner relationships, rather than receiving a separate allocation for every department.
Produce the analytical synthesis of pack coverage, claims, actions, recurring themes and support needs.
Use the agreed synthesis across King's Edge and related projects without acting as its producer.

enablingConditions.condition: Careers produces and distributes packs through BAU, programmes organise their own reviews, and course-level actions enter CER or the documented alternative route.
enablingConditions.owner: Careers and Employability Service, programmes and Academic Quality
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The broader rollout may retain project dependency rather than operating as normal practice.

enablingConditions.condition: Course teams decide local participation and use local time. The central project does not prescribe or cost course-review participation.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The resource plan could imply central ownership of activity that belongs to programmes.

enablingConditions.condition: Faculty education committees monitor local follow-through and ASQES receives an aggregate view through existing quality-enhancement reporting.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Actions and recurring support needs may not remain visible through existing oversight.

enablingConditions.condition: Any continuing BAU LLM requirement is assessed and funded separately from the £1,000 project allocation.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Unfunded BAU usage could be assumed from a time-limited project budget.

decisions.title: Confirm the remaining in-scope course population
decisions.summary: Agree the course population for the broader rollout and record any approved exceptions.

decisions.title: Confirm BAU support and exception route
decisions.summary: Confirm how programmes request Careers Consultant help, how data or interpretation questions are routed, and how completion exceptions are resolved.

decisions.title: Confirm final synthesis and feed-through
decisions.summary: Careers produces the analytical synthesis; the Project Manager coordinates inputs and evidence; Daniel Robson owns interpretation and conclusions; faculties validate themes; and Mark Peace uses the agreed synthesis across King's Edge.

decisions.title: Confirm consent for visible examples
decisions.summary: Agree how course ownership and consent will be handled where examples or stories are shared in student-facing, applicant-facing or recruitment-facing contexts.

risks.title: The final phase becomes a coverage exercise
risks.mitigation: Emphasise meaningful engagement with graduate futures evidence, with different routes depending on context, need and capacity.

risks.title: Self-review becomes abandonment
risks.mitigation: Provide clear materials, interpretation guidance and a route for help where evidence is unclear or sensitive.

risks.title: Synthesis flattens disciplinary difference
